content_hints.description: The Yawning Portal's common room is built around a forty-foot-wide well shaft that drops into absolute darkness. A pulley system lowers adventurers into Undermountain for one gold piece per head -- Durnan pulls them back up for free, provided they are still alive. The tavern does brisk business in last meals and farewell drinks. A chalkboard behind the bar tracks the current record for deepest level reached and survived: Level 14, set six years ago. No one has beaten it.

npcs.name: Durnan
npcs.dialogue: One gold each, down and back. I will wait four hours. After that, you are on your own. Any questions? Good. I do not answer questions. Step onto the platform.
npcs.notes: Retired adventurer, owner of the Yawning Portal. Over 200 years old (kept young by magic). Has been to the bottom of Undermountain and back. Speaks in clipped sentences. Judges adventurers by their gear -- good boots matter more than good swords, he says.

hazards.name: Open well shaft (140-foot drop into Undermountain)

event_triggers: On approach to well: Durnan holds out his hand. 'Gold first. Prayers second.'
On rumor: A drunken dwarf mutters about hearing his grandfather's forge hammer echoing from Level 6 -- impossible, since his grandfather died three centuries ago.

content_hints.description: The descent platform is a wooden cage reinforced with iron bands, large enough for six. Durnan works the winch with the ease of long practice, and the tavern recedes above you -- candlelight shrinking to a distant star, then nothing. The shaft walls change from brick to raw stone to something older, and the temperature drops with every foot. The rope creaks. The darkness is absolute.
event_triggers: On descent: The sounds of the tavern fade. For thirty seconds, there is only the creak of rope and the drip of water. Then the platform hits stone with a hollow boom.

content_hints.description: The landing chamber is a dome of worked stone lit by everburning torches in iron brackets. The floor is smooth -- worn down by centuries of boots, hooves, and less identifiable appendages. Three passages lead deeper, each marked with faded paint: red for 'Known Paths,' blue for 'The River,' and a third mark that has been scratched out. Graffiti covers the walls -- warnings, boasts, final messages, and one recurring phrase carved in a dozen hands: 'HALASTER SEES.'

loot.name: Adventurer's Journal (abandoned, last entry reads 'Level 3. Something following us. It has too many arms.')

event_triggers: On entry: A faint vibration in the stone -- rhythmic, like breathing. The mountain is alive.

content_hints.description: A shallow alcove has been converted into a supply cache by an enterprising halfling who hauls goods down from the surface twice a week. Rope, pitons, torches, rations, and healing potions are arranged on a folding table. The prices are extortionate and non-negotiable. A sign reads: 'NO CREDIT. NO REFUNDS. NO EXCEPTIONS. YES, THAT MEANS YOU.'

npcs.name: Tasselgryn 'Tass' Velldarn
npcs.dialogue: Torch? Two gold. Rope? Five gold. Healing potion? Twenty gold. You think that is expensive? Try buying one on Level 8 when you are bleeding out. Suddenly my prices look very reasonable.
npcs.notes: Halfling merchant. Fearless entrepreneur. Has been robbed seven times and always comes back. Keeps a loaded crossbow under the table and a Ring of Invisibility for emergencies. Knows more about the first three levels than most adventurers who have explored them.

content_hints.description: A ten-foot stone statue of a robed figure stands at the junction of three passages, one hand raised in warning or greeting -- impossible to tell which. The statue's face has been chiseled smooth, its features erased by someone who did not want it recognized. At its base, offerings have been left by passing adventurers: coins, flowers (long dead), and a child's wooden toy. A Detect Magic spell reveals the statue radiates faint divination magic. It is watching.

loot.name: Offering Coins (3 gp in copper and silver)

hazards.name: Scrying sensor (Halaster observes through the statue)

event_triggers: On Detect Magic: The statue's eyes flare briefly. A voice echoes from nowhere: 'How polite of you to announce yourselves.' Then silence.
On offering left: The statue's hand shifts slightly, as if accepting the gift. Perception DC 18 to notice.
